evidenceBoundary: These vendor baselines are derived from public documentation and are not runtime benchmark results for those vendors. DSG evidence is production-tested separately. Do not claim DSG beat a vendor unless that vendor has been runtime-tested with the same suite.

capabilities.webhookTrigger: Catch Hook / Catch Raw Hook can trigger Zaps from webhooks.
capabilities.httpMethods: Catch Hook is documented for GET, PUT, or POST API endpoints.
capabilities.endpointModel: Webhook URL is provided in the Zap editor for that Zap.

capabilities.webhookTrigger: Webhook node receives requests and can trigger workflows.
capabilities.testAndProductionUrls: Webhook node has Test URL and Production URL.
capabilities.productionExecutionHistory: Production execution data can be viewed in the Executions tab.

capabilities.customWebhook: Custom webhooks create a URL that external apps or services can call.
capabilities.scenarioExecution: Webhooks can trigger scenario execution when the URL receives a request.
capabilities.defaultResponses: Documented default responses include 200 accepted, 400 queue full, and 429 rate limit.

capabilities.apiPlatform: API Platform APIs manage endpoints, collections, clients, and access profiles.
capabilities.rateLimit: API platform endpoints are documented at up to 10 requests per second.
capabilities.limits: API platform limits include synchronous job timeout, payload, header, cache, and response/cache constraints.

capabilities.durableExecution: Temporal Workflows capture state and can resume after failures.
capabilities.workflowOrchestration: Temporal is positioned as a reliable application / durable execution platform.
capabilities.webhookModel: Temporal is not a public webhook endpoint by default; HTTP benchmark requires a wrapper endpoint.
